questions.stem: This blood-feeding insect, much smaller than a mosquito, is the vector for which infection?

explanation.summary: Female phlebotomine sandflies transmit Leishmania spp.; mosquitoes transmit malaria and reduviid bugs transmit Chagas disease.
explanation.whyCorrect: Female phlebotomine sandflies transmit Leishmania spp.; mosquitoes transmit malaria and reduviid bugs transmit Chagas disease.

questions.stem: These three light-microscopy forms (cyst, ameboid, and flagellated) belong to which free-living organism responsible for a nearly always fatal CNS infection?

explanation.summary: N. fowleri uniquely exists as trophozoite, transient flagellate, and cyst forms; it causes primary amebic meningoencephalitis.
explanation.whyCorrect: N. fowleri uniquely exists as trophozoite, transient flagellate, and cyst forms; it causes primary amebic meningoencephalitis.

questions.stem: This tissue section shows a cyst packed with slow-dividing organisms (arrows). Which parasite forms these latent tissue cysts that reactivate with immunosuppression?

explanation.summary: T. gondii persists as bradyzoite-filled tissue cysts that reactivate when cell-mediated immunity wanes.
explanation.whyCorrect: T. gondii persists as bradyzoite-filled tissue cysts that reactivate when cell-mediated immunity wanes.

questions.stem: This Giemsa-stained CSF from a patient with rapidly progressive meningoencephalitis after freshwater swimming shows a motile trophozoite (arrow). What is the organism?

explanation.summary: Motile amebic trophozoites in CSF after warm freshwater exposure indicate N. fowleri primary amebic meningoencephalitis.
explanation.whyCorrect: Motile amebic trophozoites in CSF after warm freshwater exposure indicate N. fowleri primary amebic meningoencephalitis.

questions.stem: This head CT of an AIDS patient with a CD4 count of 75 shows multiple ring-enhancing lesions. Which opportunistic parasite is the most likely cause?

explanation.summary: Multiple ring-enhancing brain lesions in an AIDS patient are classic for reactivated cerebral toxoplasmosis.
explanation.whyCorrect: Multiple ring-enhancing brain lesions in an AIDS patient are classic for reactivated cerebral toxoplasmosis.

questions.stem: A newborn has this non-contrast head CT showing scattered intracranial calcifications, along with chorioretinitis and hydrocephalus. Which congenital infection is most likely?

explanation.summary: The classic triad of chorioretinitis, hydrocephalus, and diffuse intracranial calcifications points to congenital toxoplasmosis.
explanation.whyCorrect: The classic triad of chorioretinitis, hydrocephalus, and diffuse intracranial calcifications points to congenital toxoplasmosis.

questions.stem: This pear-shaped, flagellated organism seen on a saline wet mount is a common cause of which clinical picture?

choices.label: D
choices.text: A skin ulcer at a bite site

explanation.summary: The motile, pear-shaped trophozoite with an undulating membrane is T. vaginalis, which causes trichomoniasis and colpitis macularis (strawberry cervix).
explanation.whyCorrect: The motile, pear-shaped trophozoite with an undulating membrane is T. vaginalis, which causes trichomoniasis and colpitis macularis (strawberry cervix).

questions.stem: This wet mount shows an epithelial cell so studded with bacteria that its borders are obscured. This finding indicates infection with which organism?

explanation.summary: A clue cell (epithelial cell coated with adherent bacteria) indicates bacterial vaginosis, associated with Gardnerella vaginalis.
explanation.whyCorrect: A clue cell (epithelial cell coated with adherent bacteria) indicates bacterial vaginosis, associated with Gardnerella vaginalis.

questions.stem: This bone marrow smear shows a macrophage filled with small round intracellular parasites, each with a nucleus and an adjacent rod-shaped kinetoplast. What is the diagnosis?

explanation.summary: Amastigotes bearing a nucleus and kinetoplast inside marrow macrophages confirm visceral leishmaniasis; Histoplasma yeasts lack a kinetoplast.
explanation.whyCorrect: Amastigotes bearing a nucleus and kinetoplast inside marrow macrophages confirm visceral leishmaniasis; Histoplasma yeasts lack a kinetoplast.

questions.stem: A 27-year-old man returning from Brazil develops this slowly enlarging, crusted ulcer with a raised border at a bug-bite site on his forearm. Which parasite is most likely responsible?

explanation.summary: A painless ulcer with a raised, indurated border at a sandfly-bite site after travel to Latin America is classic cutaneous leishmaniasis.
explanation.whyCorrect: A painless ulcer with a raised, indurated border at a sandfly-bite site after travel to Latin America is classic cutaneous leishmaniasis.

questions.stem: On speculum exam this cervix shows punctate hemorrhages giving a 'strawberry' appearance, accompanied by a frothy discharge and vaginal pH above 4.5. Which organism is responsible?

explanation.summary: The strawberry cervix with frothy malodorous discharge and elevated pH is characteristic of T. vaginalis.
explanation.whyCorrect: The strawberry cervix with frothy malodorous discharge and elevated pH is characteristic of T. vaginalis.

questions.stem: This crusted skin ulcer with a raised border developed weeks after a sandfly bite in an endemic region. Which disease does it represent?

explanation.summary: A chronic, relatively painless ulcer with a raised, rolled border at a sandfly-bite site is cutaneous leishmaniasis.
explanation.whyCorrect: A chronic, relatively painless ulcer with a raised, rolled border at a sandfly-bite site is cutaneous leishmaniasis.

questions.stem: This child from an endemic area has fever, wasting, and a grossly distended abdomen from massive splenomegaly ('black fever'). Which form of leishmaniasis is this?

explanation.summary: Visceral leishmaniasis (kala-azar), from L. donovani/L. infantum, causes hepatosplenomegaly, fever, wasting, and pancytopenia.
explanation.whyCorrect: Visceral leishmaniasis (kala-azar), from L. donovani/L. infantum, causes hepatosplenomegaly, fever, wasting, and pancytopenia.

questions.stem: Years after a healed skin ulcer, this patient develops progressive destruction of the nasal and oral mucosa. Which form of leishmaniasis is shown?

explanation.summary: Mucocutaneous leishmaniasis (often L. braziliensis) destroys nasopharyngeal mucosa years after the primary cutaneous lesion.
explanation.whyCorrect: Mucocutaneous leishmaniasis (often L. braziliensis) destroys nasopharyngeal mucosa years after the primary cutaneous lesion.

questions.stem: This life-cycle diagram shows an organism entering the host through the nasal passage after freshwater exposure. By what route does it reach the brain?

choices.label: B
choices.text: Retrograde travel up the spinal cord

choices.label: C
choices.text: Across the blood-brain barrier from the gut

choices.label: D
choices.text: Via cranial nerve VII from the middle ear

choices.label: E
choices.text: Along the olfactory nerve through the cribriform plate

explanation.summary: N. fowleri ascends the olfactory nerve and crosses the cribriform plate to cause primary amebic meningoencephalitis.
explanation.whyCorrect: N. fowleri ascends the olfactory nerve and crosses the cribriform plate to cause primary amebic meningoencephalitis.

questions.stem: In this life cycle, which animal serves as the definitive host where sexual reproduction and oocyst shedding occur?

explanation.summary: Felids are the definitive host of T. gondii and shed oocysts in feces; humans are intermediate hosts.
explanation.whyCorrect: Felids are the definitive host of T. gondii and shed oocysts in feces; humans are intermediate hosts.

questions.stem: This schematic shows a parasite adhering to and invading a host cell, replicating intracellularly, then triggering egress and cell lysis. Which rapidly dividing form of Toxoplasma is depicted?

explanation.summary: The invasive, rapidly replicating form that lyses host cells during acute infection is the tachyzoite; bradyzoites are the latent cyst form.
explanation.whyCorrect: The invasive, rapidly replicating form that lyses host cells during acute infection is the tachyzoite; bradyzoites are the latent cyst form.
